title: toxicodendron nodosum
intro: Toxicodendron nodosum, also known as the poison oak plant, is a type of flowering plant in the Anacardiaceae family, native to North America. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic compound that causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in many people. Despite its toxic properties, some herbal preparations of Toxicodendron nodosum have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin conditions and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically used topically, in the form of salves or ointments, to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and minor cuts and scrapes.
